The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF BERNARD M FOSTON SR
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/10/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The final court accounting of the conservator following the death of the conservatee shall include a court accounting for the period that ended on the date of death and a separate accounting for the period subsequent to the date of death. [Probate Code Section 2620(b).] (2) The petition for the approval of a final account must state the name of the personal representative of the deceased conservatee's estate if one has been appointed. If probate proceedings have not been commenced and delivery of the assets may be made pursuant to Probate Code Sect. 13100, the names of those persons entitled to the delivery of the assets must be set forth and original 13100 affidavits must be on file. The order settling the final account must provide for delivery of any remaining assets to the named personal representative or to the devisees or heirs as indicated in the petition, who are the successors of the deceased conservatee proceeding under Probate Code Sect. 13100. [LRSF 14.47 H.5.] (3) As of January 2008, the Judicial Council form Summary of Account must be used for all conservatorship accounts. [Prob. C. Sect. 2620(a) and CRC 7.575.] (4) The summary of account shown in paragraph 10 of the report does not agree with the accounting attached as Exhibit A. (5) Was there an order authorizing an allowance for which no detailed accounting was required? (6) There were disbursements for auto insurance and auto parts, but no automobile in the conservatorship estate. (7) There was also a disbursement for a safe deposit box rental, but no tangible personal property or an explanation as to why a safe deposit box was necessary. (8) Schedule C (Disbursements) does not reflect any payment of the probate referee's commissions. (9) If time was billed by a paralegal, a fee declaration must include a statement addressing his/her qualifications and compliance with Business & Professions Code Section 6450. (10) The copies of the bank statements filed on 2/26/24 were just for the Bank of Stockton account and the last one was for the period ending 7/5/23, preceding, not encompassing the ending date of the accounting period, 7/6/23. (11) No statements have been filed for the two other banks. (12) The proof of service filed on 3/5/24 is incomplete at item 4. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 5/30/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUN WAH MOON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/10/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The "complete attachment 8" attached to the fifth supplement is NOT complete. The relationship to the decedent of each person must be set forth. If a person is not within the first degree to the decedent, the deceased person through whom the second degree (or more remote) relation is related must be set forth. (2) If Frank Toy Moon's will has not yet been admitted to probate, the person named as his executor has no authority to act. He is NOT the executor until appointed by a court. (3) A Probate Code Section 13100 affidavit must be filed conventionally. [LRSF 14.59 C.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues by 5/30/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Petitioner is strongly urged to hire an attorney.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSA OSALVO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Procedural issues on Lakhsmi's Petition: (1) Petition to set forth if the decedent's will is self-proving, in #3.f.(2). (2) Proposed order must be submitted Procedural issues on Depak's Petition: (1) There have been two First Amended Petitions on file. 1/22/2024 and 1/26/2024. Need clarification if the later document must be the "Second" Amended Petition. (2) Proposed personal representative appears to be Administrator with Will Annexed not Executor because it is unknown if the purported lost will named himself as executor. (3) A copy of the lost will to be provided. It needs to be attached to Petition. Also, a copy of the lost will need to be submitted to the clerk's office for the purpose of admission. (4) Need allegation on the lost will pursuant to Prob. Code sec. 8223. Attachment 3F(3) alleges the will was not in the decedent's possession; however, petition does not provide any supportive facts or proof. (5) Notice of Petition to Administer Estate needs to be served on all persons titled. sec. 8110. The notice filed on 1/22/2024; yet it does not include the notice of direction of remote appearance. LRSF 14.1.C.6) (6) Petition to suggest an appropriate bond amount of $1,100,000. First Amended Petition filed 1/22/2024 requests $100,000 bond be ordered (#2.d.(2)); however, full bond of $1,100,000 is required. Prob. Code sec. 8482(a). If Petition seeks no IAEA, the court may require a minimum bond. Grant wIPTAHoURtT hIEaERS APIPnEG.ARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

If Petition seeks no IAEA, the court may require a minimum bond. Grant wIPTAHoURtT hIEaERS APIPnEG.ARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEPHEN GO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/3/2024. The following issues to be cleared two weeks prior to the date: (1) Need an authority under which the court can grant the transfer of the asset to the surviving spouse directly when the decedent's will expressly leaves the asset to the trust. Probate Code sec. 13501. (2) Notice of Hearing as well as Notice of Directions for Remote Appearance must be given to all persons entitled to. Prob. Code sec. 13655. LRSF. 14.1.C.6). (3) Need clarification if petition seeks determination of property passing to petitioner without administration as well as confirmation of property belonging to the petitioner. Petition 1 and 7 do not mirror each other. (4) Need Attachment 7.a. and/or 7.b depending on the relief that petition prays for. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YU XIU AI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/13/24 at counsel's request. The following issues to be cleared seven days prior to the hearing: The matter will be continued to the next available court date unless the following issues to be cleared by the end of 4/24/2024. (1) Need to supplement/amend the Petition as to the bond if petition now seeks bond to be waived. Petition #2.d. suggests a bond of $1,045,800; (2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [Form DE-147S] to be conventionally filed. See the Local Rule of SF 14.18.E and 14.59.C. (3) Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances and Access must be given, too. LRSF 14.1.C.6).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICIA JEAN HALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the sole purpose of admitting the will dated 3/8/24. [Probate Code Section 8225.] No appearance is required. Status of closing the estate will be set for 5/12/25. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is filed at least two weeks before 5/12/25, no appearance will be required on that date.
